title: Palace of Lite
content:
{{Top|ncl}}
{{Noncanon|legends=1}}
[[File:PalaceOfLite-TSW1.jpg|thumb|Palace of Lite]]
The '''Palace of Lite''' was the royal [[palace]] on [[Aquilae (The Star Wars)|Aquilae]]. It stood in the desert of that planet, and was typified by turrets, rooftop gardens and fountains.

It held a library, dining chamber, courtyard and a control center. When the [[New Galactic Empire]] invaded the [[Aquilaean system]], the royal family fled to their [[Underground Fortress]], and the palace was occupied by [[Valorum (The Star Wars)|Valorum]] and the Imperial military.

It survived the battles over Aquilae, and was the site of the investiture of the new [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Leia (The Star Wars)|Leia]] and the ceremony that rewarded the heroic [[Annikin Starkiller]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], [[R2-D2 (The Star Wars)|Artwo]] and [[C-3PO (The Star Wars)|Threepio]].

==Behind the scenes==
Eventually the Palace of Lite became the [[Grand Audience Chamber]] of the [[Great Temple/Legends|Massassi Temple]] in the final version of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']].

The Palace of Lite itself eventually had an appearance of sorts in the [[Dark Horse Comics]] adaptation of the rough draft, titled ''[[The Star Wars]]'', released in 2013. The writer, [[Jonathan W. Rinzler]], in a PDF file giving a brief transcript of pages 14 and 15 of the then-nearly finished first issue, mentioned that he, just for fun and to give a more Arabic look to the palace, had planned to base the design of the Palace of Lite on that of Agrabah from the 1992 [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] classic ''Aladdin'', although he also stated that whether that design choice would be retained would be up to [[George Lucas]].
